title: xantolis psammophila
intro: Xantolis psammophila, also known as the sand-bush, is a type of plant species in the family Sapotaceae. It is traditionally used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Xantolis psammophila include its potential to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its ability to aid in wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves are often used to make infusions or decoctions, and its roots are sometimes used to create tonics or extracts, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
